## 2. Electron + electron-vite Secure Project Setup

### 2.1 Core security model

- The three security flags compose cleanly and are all **Electron defaults** in modern versions: `contextIsolation: true`, `nodeIntegration: false`, `sandbox: true`. Keep them on.
- Electron 42 can emit true ESM for main/preload вЂ” **but a sandboxed preload should still be CJS** (see below).
- Prefer the **CSP HTTP header** (`session...onHeadersReceived`) over a `<meta>` tag (`<meta>` can't be used over `file://` anyway).
- Load large PDF bytes via a **custom standard+privileged protocol** (`protocol.handle`), not IPC `ArrayBuffer` transfer and not `file://`.

### 2.2 Why a sandboxed preload must be CJS and fully bundled

1. **Sandbox removes the Node runtime from the preload.** Since Electron 20, preload scripts are sandboxed by default. A sandboxed preload gets a *polyfilled* `require` exposing only a small allow-list (`electron`, a few helpers) вЂ” not Node's real loader. `require('some-dep')` or loading a second emitted chunk fails at runtime: `Error: module not found: 'foo'`.
2. **electron-vite always builds the preload as CJS.** A preload must load the `electron` module regardless of main's node integration; CJS reliably works. With `"type": "module"` on an ESM-capable Electron, electron-vite *may* emit ESM `.mjs`; when it falls back to CJS it emits **`.cjs`** вЂ” reference the preload with its `.cjs` extension in `webPreferences.preload`.
3. **CJS code-splitting is the failure mode.** Rollup emitting `cjs` splits into chunks linked by `require()`; under sandbox those inter-chunk calls fail. electron-vite already configures Rollup to **not** split the preload (single file) вЂ” handled for you.
4. **The remaining gotcha is `externalizeDepsPlugin`.** electron-vite's default externalizes everything in `dependencies` (good for *main*, which has full Node). For a **sandboxed preload**, externalized deps become runtime `require('dep')` calls the sandbox can't resolve. **Fix: do NOT apply `externalizeDepsPlugin` in the `preload` block** so all preload deps inline into the single bundle. Keep it on `main`.

> вљ пёЏ Verify: The "drop `externalizeDepsPlugin` for preload" guidance comes from electron-vite maintainer discussion #423, not a numbered doc section. The underlying mechanism (sandbox can't `require` external files) is well-documented. Safest practice: any dependency used inside the preload at runtime must be bundled in; a preload with *zero* third-party deps (only `electron`) makes externalization moot.

### 2.3 `electron.vite.config.ts` (React + TS)

```ts
// electron.vite.config.ts
import { resolve } from 'node:path'
import { defineConfig, externalizeDepsPlugin } from 'electron-vite'
import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'

export default defineConfig({
  main: {
    // Main has full Node: externalizing native/3rd-party deps is fine & faster.
    plugins: [externalizeDepsPlugin()],
    build: {
      rollupOptions: {
        input: { index: resolve(__dirname, 'src/main/index.ts') }
      }
    }
  },
  preload: {
    // SANDBOX-SAFE: NO externalizeDepsPlugin here.
    // All preload deps must be bundled into one CJS file the sandbox can load.
    build: {
      rollupOptions: {
        input: { index: resolve(__dirname, 'src/preload/index.ts') }
        // electron-vite already forces CJS + no code-splitting for preload.
      }
    }
  },
  renderer: {
    // Standard Vite web app вЂ” never use Node APIs here.
    plugins: [react()],
    build: {
      rollupOptions: {
        input: { index: resolve(__dirname, 'src/renderer/index.html') }
      }
    }
  }
})
```

With the zero-config layout (`src/main/index.ts`, `src/preload/index.ts`, `src/renderer/index.html`), the `rollupOptions.input` entries can be omitted вЂ” electron-vite auto-detects them.

`package.json` essentials:

```jsonc
{
  "main": "out/main/index.js",      // or out/main/index.cjs if CJS-emitted
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "electron-vite dev",
    "build": "electron-vite build",
    "start": "electron-vite preview",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit"
  }
}
```

> вљ пёЏ Verify: If `"type": "module"` is set and the preload emits as `.cjs`, set `webPreferences.preload` to the `...preload/index.cjs` path. Check the actual emitted extension in `out/preload/` after a build rather than assuming.

### 2.4 Window creation (all three flags on)

```ts
// src/main/index.ts
import { app, BrowserWindow, shell } from 'electron'
import { join } from 'node:path'

function createWindow() {
  const win = new BrowserWindow({
    webPreferences: {
      preload: join(__dirname, '../preload/index.js'), // or .cjs вЂ” see note
      contextIsolation: true,   // default true вЂ” keep it
      nodeIntegration: false,   // default false вЂ” keep it
      sandbox: true,            // default true вЂ” keep it
      webSecurity: true,        // never disable in production
      allowRunningInsecureContent: false
    }
  })

  // Block in-page navigation to external origins
  win.webContents.on('will-navigate', (event, url) => {
    const allowed = is.dev ? 'http://localhost:5173' : 'app://' // your origin
    if (!url.startsWith(allowed)) event.preventDefault()
  })

  // Block window.open / target=_blank; route safe https to the OS browser
  win.webContents.setWindowOpenHandler(({ url }) => {
    if (url.startsWith('https://')) shell.openExternal(url) // vet/allowlist first
    return { action: 'deny' }
  })

  return win
}
```

`shell.openExternal` safe pattern: only ever pass URLs validated against an allow-list of schemes/hosts (`https:` only, known domains). Never forward arbitrary renderer-supplied strings вЂ” `openExternal` can launch arbitrary protocol handlers.

### 2.5 contextBridge вЂ” minimal typed API, no `ipcRenderer` leak

```ts
// src/preload/index.ts
import { contextBridge, ipcRenderer } from 'electron'

const api = {
  // one method per channel вЂ” never expose ipcRenderer or generic send()
  loadPrefs: (): Promise<Prefs> => ipcRenderer.invoke('prefs:load'),
  savePrefs: (p: Prefs): Promise<void> => ipcRenderer.invoke('prefs:save', p),

  // Listener: wrap the callback so `event` (and thus event.sender в†’ ipcRenderer)
  // is NEVER passed to the renderer.
  onProgress: (cb: (pct: number) => void) => {
    const handler = (_e: unknown, pct: number) => cb(pct)
    ipcRenderer.on('job:progress', handler)
    return () => ipcRenderer.removeListener('job:progress', handler) // unsubscribe
  }
} as const

export type Api = typeof api

if (process.contextIsolated) {
  contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld('api', api)
} else {
  // contextIsolation must be on; this branch should never run in your build
  ;(window as unknown as { api: Api }).api = api
}
```

Renderer-side typing:

```ts
// src/renderer/src/env.d.ts
import type { Api } from '../../preload'
declare global {
  interface Window { api: Api }
}
export {}
```

Rules confirmed by Electron docs: modern Electron **blocks sending `ipcRenderer` whole** over the bridge; never expose `ipcRenderer.send`/`.on` directly; for listeners, **wrap to strip the event arg** (otherwise you leak `ipcRenderer` via `event.sender`). A generic `invoke(channel, вЂ¦)` bridge MUST allow-list channels or it reintroduces arbitrary IPC.

### 2.6 Strict CSP (dev vs production) via HTTP header

```ts
// src/main/csp.ts
import { session } from 'electron'

const DEV = process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development' || !!process.env.ELECTRON_RENDERER_URL

// Dev MUST allow inline + eval + the Vite HMR websocket/server.
const DEV_CSP = [
  "default-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' data:",
  "script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval'",
  "style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'",
  "connect-src 'self' ws: http://localhost:5173",
  "img-src 'self' data: blob:"
].join('; ')

// Production: NO unsafe-inline / NO unsafe-eval.
const PROD_CSP = [
  "default-src 'none'",
  "script-src 'self'",
  "style-src 'self'",                 // add a nonce if you have inline styles
  "img-src 'self' data: blob: app:",  // 'app:' = your custom protocol below
  "font-src 'self'",
  "connect-src 'self' app:",
  "object-src 'none'",
  "base-uri 'none'",
  "frame-ancestors 'none'"
].join('; ')

export function installCsp() {
  session.defaultSession.webRequest.onHeadersReceived((details, cb) => {
    cb({
      responseHeaders: {
        ...details.responseHeaders,
        'Content-Security-Policy': [DEV ? DEV_CSP : PROD_CSP]
      }
    })
  })
}
```

Gotchas:
- **Vite HMR in dev** needs `'unsafe-inline'` (injected styles), `'unsafe-eval'` (React Fast Refresh / some transforms), and `connect-src ws:` for the HMR socket. Scope strictly to dev.
- Production `style-src 'self'` breaks runtime-injected inline styles; use a nonce/hash rather than `'unsafe-inline'` if you must keep them.
- Advisory **GHSA-4p4r-m79c-wq3v** (HTTP response-header injection) affects apps that reflect untrusted input into `onHeadersReceived`/`protocol.handle` response headers. Never interpolate renderer input into header names/values. (Fixed in 38.8.6 / 39.8.3 / 40.8.3 / 41.0.3; Electron 42 is clear.)
- For OCR: ensure CSP allows blob/worker (`worker-src blob:`) since tesseract.js `workerBlobURL` defaults to `true`. See В§5.

### 2.7 Loading large PDF bytes via custom protocol (recommended)

Use a **standard + secure + fetch-supporting** custom scheme with `protocol.handle`. This streams bytes through Chromium's loader (efficient, range-request friendly), keeps `webSecurity` intact, avoids `file://` over-exposure, and avoids copying a multi-MB `ArrayBuffer` across the contextBridge.

```ts
// src/main/protocol.ts
import { app, protocol, net } from 'electron'
import { pathToFileURL } from 'node:url'
import { join, normalize } from 'node:path'

// MUST run before app 'ready'
protocol.registerSchemesAsPrivileged([
  {
    scheme: 'app',
    privileges: { standard: true, secure: true, supportFetchAPI: true, stream: true }
  }
])

const PDF_ROOT = /* absolute dir you control */ app.getPath('userData')

export function installPdfProtocol() {
  protocol.handle('app', async (request) => {
    const { hostname, pathname } = new URL(request.url) // e.g. app://pdf/doc.pdf
    if (hostname !== 'pdf') return new Response('not found', { status: 404 })

    // Path-traversal guard: resolve & confirm it stays under PDF_ROOT
    const target = normalize(join(PDF_ROOT, pathname))
    if (!target.startsWith(normalize(PDF_ROOT))) {
      return new Response('forbidden', { status: 403 })
    }

    // Delegate to net.fetch for streaming + range support
    return net.fetch(pathToFileURL(target).toString(), {
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/pdf' }
    })
  })
}
```

Renderer points at it: `<iframe src="app://pdf/doc.pdf">` or pdf.js with URL `app://pdf/doc.pdf`. `app:` is whitelisted in the production CSP (`img-src`/`connect-src`). Prefer IPC `ArrayBuffer` only for *small* or *ephemeral, never-on-disk* blobs. If the window uses a custom `partition`, register the handler on `session.fromPartition(...)`, not the default session.

### 2.8 zod validation at the IPC boundary

```ts
// src/main/ipc.ts
import { ipcMain, type IpcMainInvokeEvent } from 'electron'
import { z, type ZodTypeAny } from 'zod'

export function handle<S extends ZodTypeAny, R>(
  channel: string,
  schema: S,
  handler: (event: IpcMainInvokeEvent, payload: z.infer<S>) => Promise<R> | R
) {
  ipcMain.handle(channel, async (event, raw) => {
    const parsed = await schema.safeParseAsync(raw)
    if (!parsed.success) {
      // Do NOT echo attacker input back into errors/headers (header-injection advisory)
      throw new Error(`Invalid payload on "${channel}"`)
    }
    // Optional: origin / frame allow-listing
    // if (new URL(event.senderFrame.url).protocol !== 'app:') throw new Error('bad origin')
    return handler(event, parsed.data)
  })
}

// usage
const SavePrefs = z.object({ theme: z.enum(['light', 'dark']), zoom: z.number().min(0.5).max(3) })
handle('prefs:save', SavePrefs, async (_e, prefs) => {
  // prefs is fully typed: { theme: 'light' | 'dark'; zoom: number }
})
```

Use Zod v4. For synchronous schemas `safeParse` is fine and avoids async overhead; use `safeParseAsync` only when a schema has async refinements/transforms (otherwise Zod throws).

## 3. PDF Rendering вЂ” pdfjs-dist (renderer)

### 3.1 Version & runtime

Current stable **`pdfjs-dist@6.0.227`** (published ~late May 2026). **ESM-only** (ships `.mjs`, no CommonJS); `engines` requires **Node `>=22.13.0 || >=24`** (driven by `Promise.withResolvers`). Fine in an Electron renderer (Electron 30+ bundles modern V8/Node). The machine running `electron-vite build` must be on Node 22.13+/24+. For older runtimes use the **legacy build** at `pdfjs-dist/legacy/build/*` (transpiled, polyfill-friendly).

> вљ пёЏ Verify: Whether legacy is strictly needed in Electron вЂ” the modern build works in current Chromium, but legacy is the safe choice for broad device support.

### 3.2 Worker wiring (the critical bit)

Two approaches work with Vite ESM:

- `new URL('pdfjs-dist/build/pdf.worker.min.mjs', import.meta.url)` вЂ” officially documented, but the hashed/relative URL resolution can be fragile in some Vite/electron-vite setups, especially under `file://` in a packaged app.
- `import workerUrl from 'pdfjs-dist/build/pdf.worker.min.mjs?url'` вЂ” the `?url` import. Generally **most reliable in Vite/electron-vite** (Vite emits the worker as a hashed asset and gives a correct URL in both dev and build).

Set `workerSrc` once, in the same module where you call `getDocument` (module-load order can otherwise reset it).

> вљ пёЏ Verify: The `?url` vs `new URL` reliability ranking is based on community reports, not an official electron-vite statement.

```ts
// src/renderer/pdf/pdfjs.ts  (recommended ?url form)
import * as pdfjsLib from 'pdfjs-dist';
import workerUrl from 'pdfjs-dist/build/pdf.worker.min.mjs?url';
pdfjsLib.GlobalWorkerOptions.workerSrc = workerUrl;
export { pdfjsLib };
```

Alternative (official `new URL`):
```ts
pdfjsLib.GlobalWorkerOptions.workerSrc =
  new URL('pdfjs-dist/build/pdf.worker.min.mjs', import.meta.url).toString();
```

### 3.3 getDocument + canvas render (HiDPI)

```ts
// Pass a *fresh* Uint8Array вЂ” pdf.js may detach/transfer the buffer to the worker.
const data = new Uint8Array(arrayBuffer); // copy if you need to keep the original
const loadingTask = pdfjsLib.getDocument({
  data,
  cMapUrl: '/cmaps/', cMapPacked: true,
  standardFontDataUrl: '/standard_fonts/',
});
const pdf = await loadingTask.promise;
const page = await pdf.getPage(1);
```

```ts
const scale = 1.5;
const viewport = page.getViewport({ scale });
const outputScale = window.devicePixelRatio || 1;

const canvas = canvasRef.current!;
const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d')!;
canvas.width  = Math.floor(viewport.width  * outputScale);  // bitmap
canvas.height = Math.floor(viewport.height * outputScale);
canvas.style.width  = `${Math.floor(viewport.width)}px`;    // CSS size
canvas.style.height = `${Math.floor(viewport.height)}px`;

const transform = outputScale !== 1 ? [outputScale, 0, 0, outputScale, 0, 0] : undefined;
const task = page.render({ canvasContext: ctx, viewport, transform });
await task.promise;
// task.cancel() on unmount/re-render; one canvas cannot host two concurrent renders.
```

> вљ пёЏ Verify: v6 is an `[api-major]` release; it is unconfirmed whether `render()` now also expects/prefers a `canvas` property alongside `canvasContext`. `canvasContext` was the documented key through 5.x. Check the installed `types/src/display/api.d.ts`.

### 3.4 Text layer (selectable/copyable) вЂ” `TextLayer` class

`renderTextLayer()` is **deprecated**; use the **`TextLayer` class**. Requires `import 'pdfjs-dist/web/pdf_viewer.css'`, a container with class `textLayer`, and the **`--total-scale-factor`** CSS variable (v5/v6; v4 used `--scale-factor`).

```ts
import { TextLayer } from 'pdfjs-dist';
import 'pdfjs-dist/web/pdf_viewer.css';

const textContent = await page.getTextContent();
const div = textLayerRef.current!;       // <div class="textLayer">
div.style.setProperty('--total-scale-factor', String(viewport.scale));

const textLayer = new TextLayer({
  textContentSource: textContent,
  container: div,
  viewport,                              // some setups clone with { dontFlip: true }
});
await textLayer.render();
// On zoom: textLayer.update({ viewport: newViewport });
```

Minimum CSS (provided by `pdf_viewer.css`):
```css
.textLayer { position: absolute; inset: 0; overflow: hidden;
  opacity: 1; line-height: 1; --total-scale-factor: 1; }
.textLayer span { color: transparent; position: absolute; white-space: pre;
  transform-origin: 0 0; cursor: text; }
.textLayer ::selection { background: rgba(0,0,255,0.25); }
```

### 3.5 Annotation layer

`AnnotationLayer` instance + `render()`, fed by `await page.getAnnotations({ intent: 'display' })`, a `linkService` (`PDFLinkService` or `SimpleLinkService`; `null` throws), and `viewport.clone({ dontFlip: true })`.

```ts
import { AnnotationLayer } from 'pdfjs-dist';
import { PDFLinkService } from 'pdfjs-dist/web/pdf_viewer.mjs';

const annotations = await page.getAnnotations({ intent: 'display' });
const annDiv = annotationLayerRef.current!;
annDiv.replaceChildren();

const layer = new AnnotationLayer({
  div: annDiv,
  page,
  viewport: viewport.clone({ dontFlip: true }),
  annotationCanvasMap: new Map(),
  structTreeLayer: null,
});
await layer.render({
  annotations,
  page,
  viewport: viewport.clone({ dontFlip: true }),
  div: annDiv,
  linkService: new PDFLinkService(),   // never null
  renderForms: false,                  // true + annotationStorage for interactive forms
});
```

### 3.6 getTextContent geometry (for Tier-2 overlays)

```ts
interface TextItem {
  str: string;
  dir: 'ttb' | 'ltr' | 'rtl';
  transform: [number, number, number, number, number, number]; // [a,b,c,d,e,f]; e=x, f=y (PDF space)
  width: number;   // device-space width
  height: number;  // see caveat вЂ” may need transform[3] scaling
  fontName: string;       // key into textContent.styles
  hasEOL: boolean;
}
// styles[fontName] => { ascent, descent, vertical, fontFamily }
```

The `height` field is unreliable. For a glyph-run bounding box, derive font size/height from the transform: `fontHeight = Math.hypot(transform[2], transform[3])`, then map `[e, f]` through `viewport.transform` to screen coordinates rather than trusting `item.height`.

### 3.7 Full-text search via `PDFFindController`

Driven entirely via `EventBus`. It walks all pages' text and highlights into the text layer, so the text layer must be rendered for highlights to appear.

```ts
import { EventBus, PDFLinkService, PDFFindController } from 'pdfjs-dist/web/pdf_viewer.mjs';

const eventBus = new EventBus();
const linkService = new PDFLinkService({ eventBus });
const findController = new PDFFindController({ linkService, eventBus });
findController.setDocument(pdf);   // after getDocument resolves

eventBus.on('updatefindmatchescount', ({ matchesCount }) => {/* {current,total} */});
eventBus.on('updatefindcontrolstate', ({ state, matchesCount }) => {/* FOUND/NOT_FOUND/PENDING */});

// new search:
eventBus.dispatch('find', {
  type: '', query: 'invoice',
  caseSensitive: false, entireWord: false,
  highlightAll: true, matchDiacritics: false, findPrevious: false,
});
// next/prev:
eventBus.dispatch('find', { type: 'again', query: 'invoice', findPrevious: false, highlightAll: true });
```

### 3.8 Large (1000-page) docs

Never render all pages. Virtualize: keep `PDFPageProxy` lazy, render only visible+buffer pages to canvas, recycle/destroy off-screen canvases, and call `page.cleanup()`. Note `PDFFindController` still indexes **all** pages' text regardless of what's rendered.

### 3.9 cMap / standard fonts copy (electron-vite)

```bash
npm i pdfjs-dist@6.0.227
```

```ts
import { defineConfig } from 'electron-vite';
import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react';
import { viteStaticCopy } from 'vite-plugin-static-copy';

export default defineConfig({
  renderer: {
    plugins: [
      react(),
      viteStaticCopy({
        targets: [
          { src: 'node_modules/pdfjs-dist/cmaps/*',          dest: 'cmaps' },
          { src: 'node_modules/pdfjs-dist/standard_fonts/*', dest: 'standard_fonts' },
        ],
      }),
    ],
  },
});
```

Pass `cMapUrl: '/cmaps/'`, `cMapPacked: true`, `standardFontDataUrl: '/standard_fonts/'` to `getDocument`. Define that options object **outside** the React component (or `useMemo`) to avoid reload loops.

> вљ пёЏ Verify: `vite-plugin-static-copy` exact latest version вЂ” install latest with `npm i -D vite-plugin-static-copy`.

### 3.10 Gotchas

- **MIME-type errors** (`Expected a JavaScript module script but the server responded with application/octet-stream`): happens when `.mjs` worker/viewer files are served raw (e.g. direct `node_modules` path under `file://` in a packaged app). Fix: let Vite bundle the worker via `?url`/`new URL` so it becomes a hashed asset.
- **Vite filename hashing** breaks hard-coded worker paths; `?url` sidesteps this. Manual copy to `public/` is a last-resort fallback.
- **electron-vite renderer externalization**: on "worker failed to load," confirm `pdfjs-dist` is bundled by the renderer build (not externalized) and `optimizeDeps` isn't pre-bundling the worker in a way that drops the asset.
- **`workerSrc` reset**: set it in the same module that calls `getDocument`.
- **Detached ArrayBuffer**: pdf.js transfers the buffer to the worker; reuse a copy if you still need the bytes.
- **CSS variable rename**: `--total-scale-factor` (v5/v6) vs old `--scale-factor` (v4). Missing it = misaligned/invisible text layer.
- **One canvas, one render**: cancel the prior `RenderTask` before re-rendering the same canvas.
- **`linkService: null`** in AnnotationLayer throws вЂ” always pass a `PDFLinkService`/`SimpleLinkService`.
- **Node engine**: build host below Node 22.13 fails to install/run v6.
- **`height` field** in text items is unreliable; compute from the transform.

- mozilla/pdf.js Releases: https://github.com/mozilla/pdf.js/releases
- PDF.js Examples (canvas render + outputScale): https://mozilla.github.io/pdf.js/examples/
- PDF.js API docs (TextItem / TextContent): https://mozilla.github.io/pdf.js/api/draft/module-pdfjsLib.html
- Issue #18206 (renderTextLayer deprecated в†’ TextLayer): https://github.com/mozilla/pdf.js/issues/18206
- pdfjs-dist 5.4.0 text-layer docs: https://tessl.io/registry/tessl/npm-pdfjs-dist/5.4.0/files/docs/text-layer.md
- Annotations and Forms вЂ” DeepWiki: https://deepwiki.com/mozilla/pdfjs-dist/5.4-annotations-and-forms
- PDFFindController text search (Nutrient): https://www.nutrient.io/blog/pdfjs-text-search-pdffindcontroller/

## 4. PDF Manipulation вЂ” pdf-lib

### 4.1 Profile

Pure-JS, MIT, dependency-light, isomorphic (browser + Node + Deno + React Native). **Strong** at *constructing* content and *manipulating document structure*. **Weak/absent** at *encryption*, *content-stream/glyph rewriting*, *true redaction*, and *rich markup annotations*.

- Confirmed `pdf-lib@1.17.1` (dist-tag `latest`), MIT, published 2022-05-12. Core effectively unmaintained.
- All async I/O (`load`, `create`, `save`, `embedFont`, `embedPng`, `embedJpg`, `copyPages`) returns **Promises** вЂ” `await` them.
- **Coordinate origin is bottom-left**, y increases upward (PDF/PostScript convention) вЂ” opposite of PDF.js/DOM. See В§4.9.

> вљ пёЏ Verify: For encryption/password-decryption use the maintained fork **`@cantoo/pdf-lib`**; confirm exact version with `npm view @cantoo/pdf-lib version`.

### 4.2 Loading & saving

```ts
import { PDFDocument } from 'pdf-lib';

const doc = await PDFDocument.create();                          // new
const doc = await PDFDocument.load(existingBytes);               // Uint8Array | ArrayBuffer | base64
// Encrypted input: load() THROWS EncryptedPDFError by default.
const doc = await PDFDocument.load(existingBytes, { ignoreEncryption: true });

const bytes = await doc.save();                                  // -> Uint8Array
const dataUri = await doc.saveAsBase64({ dataUri: true });       // base64 data URI
```

`LoadOptions`: `ignoreEncryption` (default `false`), `parseSpeed` (`ParseSpeeds | number`), `throwOnInvalidObject`, `updateMetadata`, `capNumbers`.
`SaveOptions`: `useObjectStreams` (default `true`), `addDefaultPage`, `objectsPerTick`, `updateFieldAppearances`.

> **Critical caveat on `ignoreEncryption: true`:** does **NOT decrypt** вЂ” it only suppresses the error. Modifying/copying pages from such a doc commonly yields **blank pages** (issues #1326, #1390). Real decryption (`PDFDocument.load(bytes, { ignoreEncryption: true, password })`) exists only in `@cantoo/pdf-lib`, not core pdf-lib.

### 4.3 Page operations

```ts
doc.getPageCount();                 // number
doc.getPages();                     // PDFPage[]
doc.getPage(index);                 // PDFPage

const page = doc.addPage();                       // default size
const page = doc.addPage([612, 792]);             // US Letter, [width,height] in pt
const page = doc.insertPage(0, [595.28, 841.89]); // A4 at index 0
doc.removePage(index);

page.getSize();                     // { width, height }
page.getWidth(); page.getHeight();
page.setSize(w, h);

import { degrees } from 'pdf-lib';
page.setRotation(degrees(90));      // must be a multiple of 90
page.getRotation();                 // -> Rotation (use .angle / degrees())
```

Merge / reorder / split via `copyPages` (you cannot share `PDFPage` objects between docs):

```ts
// MERGE docB into docA
const copied = await docA.copyPages(docB, docB.getPageIndices()); // PDFPage[]
copied.forEach(p => docA.addPage(p));

// REORDER within one doc: copy into a fresh doc in the desired order
const out = await PDFDocument.create();
const newOrder = [2, 0, 1];
const pages = await out.copyPages(src, newOrder);
pages.forEach(p => out.addPage(p));

// SPLIT: one output doc per page
for (const i of src.getPageIndices()) {
  const sub = await PDFDocument.create();
  const [pg] = await sub.copyPages(src, [i]);
  sub.addPage(pg);
  await sub.save();
}
```

`doc.getPageIndices()` returns `[0..n-1]`. There is no single `mergePdf()`/`split()` helper вЂ” the `copyPages` + `addPage`/`insertPage` pattern *is* the idiom.

### 4.4 Drawing

```ts
import { StandardFonts, rgb, degrees } from 'pdf-lib';

const helv = await doc.embedFont(StandardFonts.Helvetica);
// Others: HelveticaBold, TimesRoman, TimesRomanBold, Courier, ZapfDingbats, Symbol, etc.

page.drawText('Hello', {
  x: 50, y: 700, size: 24, font: helv,
  color: rgb(0, 0.4, 0.9),
  rotate: degrees(0),
  lineHeight: 28, maxWidth: 400,
});

// Custom fonts (TTF/OTF) require fontkit:
import fontkit from '@pdf-lib/fontkit';
doc.registerFontkit(fontkit);
const custom = await doc.embedFont(fontBytes, { subset: true }); // subset reduces size

// Text measurement
helv.widthOfTextAtSize('Hello', 24);
helv.heightAtSize(24);

// Images
const png = await doc.embedPng(pngBytes);    // PDFImage
const jpg = await doc.embedJpg(jpgBytes);
const dims = png.scale(0.5);
page.drawImage(png, { x: 100, y: 400, width: dims.width, height: dims.height });

// Vector shapes
page.drawRectangle({ x, y, width, height,
  color: rgb(1,0,0), borderColor: rgb(0,0,0), borderWidth: 1, opacity: 0.5, rotate: degrees(0) });
page.drawEllipse({ x, y, xScale, yScale, color: rgb(0,0,1) });
page.drawCircle({ x, y, size, color });
page.drawLine({ start: {x,y}, end: {x,y}, thickness: 2, color: rgb(0,0,0), opacity: 1 });
page.drawSquare({ x, y, size });
page.drawSvgPath(pathString, { x, y, scale, color, borderColor });
```

Notes: PNG and JPEG are the **only** built-in image embedders (no GIF/WebP/BMP вЂ” convert first). `embedPng` supports alpha. `embedFont` only embeds **TrueType/OpenType** via fontkit; Type1 custom fonts are not supported.

### 4.5 Annotations вЂ” what pdf-lib can / cannot do

**Core fact:** pdf-lib has **no high-level API for markup annotations** (Highlight, Ink, Text/sticky-note, Underline, StrikeOut, Squiggly, Stamp, Link, Popup). `drawText`/`drawRectangle`/etc. write directly into the **content stream** вЂ” they are *painted page content*, not interactive annotation objects, so they won't appear in a viewer's comments panel and can't be selected/edited/deleted as annotations.

- **Widget annotations (`/Widget`)**: created by pdf-lib only as the visual representation of **AcroForm fields** (see В§4.6). No general widget-creation API outside forms.
- **Real markup annotations**: a genuine `/Highlight` or `/Ink` requires hand-building the annotation dictionary with the low-level API (`doc.context.obj({...})`, `doc.context.register(...)`) and pushing the ref onto the page's `/Annots` array (`page.node.addAnnot(ref)` / manipulate `page.node.Annots()`). **No documented helper**; unsupported, manual, error-prone (appearance streams, `QuadPoints`, etc.).
- **What must be flattened instead**: anything you want to render identically everywhere вЂ” form fields and any pseudo-annotation appearance вЂ” should be flattened into page content via `form.flatten()` (fields) or simply drawn directly. After flattening, objects are no longer interactive.

**Recommendation for Verso:** implement highlight/ink/box/freehand markup by **drawing into the content stream** (vector + opacity) and tracking them in your own app model, OR delegate genuine interactive-annotation authoring to a dedicated lib (PDF.js annotation layer for display, or a commercial SDK). pdf-lib alone cannot round-trip real markup annotations.

### 4.6 AcroForm fields

```ts
const form = doc.getForm();           // PDFForm

// Lookup by name
form.getTextField('first_name');      // PDFTextField
form.getCheckBox('agree');            // PDFCheckBox
form.getRadioGroup('color');          // PDFRadioGroup
form.getDropdown('country');          // PDFDropdown
form.getOptionList('langs');          // PDFOptionList
form.getButton('submit');             // PDFButton
form.getFields();                     // PDFField[]
field.getName();                      // string

// Reading
textField.getText();                  // string | undefined
checkBox.isChecked();                 // boolean
radioGroup.getSelected();             // string | undefined
radioGroup.getOptions();              // string[]
dropdown.getSelected();               // string[]
optionList.getSelected();             // string[]

// Setting
textField.setText('Jane');
textField.setMaxLength(40); textField.enableMultiline();
checkBox.check(); checkBox.uncheck();
radioGroup.select('Blue');
dropdown.select('USA'); dropdown.addOptions(['USA','Canada']); dropdown.setOptions([...]);
optionList.select(['EN','FR']);

// Create programmatically
const tf = form.createTextField('new.field');
tf.setText('hi');
tf.addToPage(page, { x, y, width, height });

// Re-render appearances (before save if values changed without flattening)
form.updateFieldAppearances(customFont);

// Flatten: bakes widget appearances into page content, removes fields + widgets. Irreversible.
form.flatten();
```

Field classes: `PDFTextField`, `PDFCheckBox`, `PDFRadioGroup`, `PDFDropdown`, `PDFOptionList`, `PDFButton` (all extend `PDFField`). Gotcha: `form.flatten()` / `updateFieldAppearances` can throw `Expected instance of PDFDict or PDFStream, but got instance of undefined` on forms with missing widget appearance dicts (issue #814) вЂ” common with some government forms; embed a font and call `updateFieldAppearances` first, and consider per-field try/catch.

### 4.7 Metadata

```ts
doc.setTitle('My Title');             // optional 2nd arg { showInWindowTitleBar: true }
doc.setAuthor('Jane Doe');
doc.setSubject('Subject');
doc.setKeywords(['a', 'b', 'c']);     // string[]
doc.setCreator('My App');
doc.setProducer('My App');
doc.setCreationDate(new Date());
doc.setModificationDate(new Date());
doc.setLanguage('en-US');

// Read:
doc.getTitle(); doc.getAuthor(); doc.getSubject(); doc.getKeywords();
doc.getCreator(); doc.getProducer(); doc.getCreationDate(); doc.getModificationDate();
```

These write the **document Info dictionary**. pdf-lib does not author full XMP metadata packets via a high-level API.

### 4.8 HARD LIMITS вЂ” confirmed pdf-lib CANNOT do these; delegate

1. **Strong encryption / password protection (output).** No API to produce an encrypted PDF. It can only *attempt* to read encrypted input (and `ignoreEncryption: true` does not decrypt). No RC4, no AES-128/256. в†’ **Delegate to `qpdf`** (see В§6).
   ```bash
   # AES-256 (256-bit always uses AES). Use a STRONG, DISTINCT owner password.
   qpdf input.pdf output.pdf --encrypt "userpw" "ownerpw" 256 --object-streams=generate --
   # Decrypt:
   qpdf --password=PW --decrypt in.pdf out.pdf
   ```
   (`@cantoo/pdf-lib` adds encrypt/decrypt in-JS вЂ” вљ пёЏ Verify its exact API surface/stability; qpdf is the authoritative, audited choice.)

2. **Content-stream / glyph editing.** pdf-lib cannot edit/remove existing text/vector content (it can only *add* content, or edit form-field text via `PDFTextField.setText`). Maintainers state editing page text is out of scope. в†’ Use **PDF.js** (`getTextContent`) or **PDFium** for extraction/reflow; **qpdf** or **mutool** for structural rewriting.

3. **True redaction.** A black `drawRectangle` over text **is not redaction** вЂ” underlying text/objects remain in the content stream and are fully extractable (copy/paste, search, `pdftotext`). в†’ For genuine redaction remove the content: **PDFium**-based tooling or **mutool**, then a sanitizing/linearizing pass with **qpdf** (`qpdf --linearize` / rewrite) to drop incremental-update remnants. See В§6 for the recommended rasterize approach.

> вљ пёЏ License watch (from В§6): for rasterizing/redaction rendering, use **PDFium** (BSD/Apache-2.0). Keep **MuPDF** and **Ghostscript** (both AGPL) out of a closed-source consumer build.

### 4.9 Coordinate system & PDF.js conversion

- **pdf-lib:** origin **bottom-left**, `+y` **up**, units = PDF points (1/72").
- **PDF.js viewport / DOM:** origin **top-left**, `+y` **down**.

Convert a PDF.js viewport point `(vx, vy)` (CSS px at scale 1, top-left) в†’ pdf-lib page coords:

```ts
// pageHeight = page.getHeight() in points (== viewport at scale 1)
// If PDF.js viewport scale != 1, divide vx,vy by scale first.
const x_pdf = vx / scale;
const y_pdf = pageHeight - (vy / scale);

// For a rectangle given top-left (vx,vy,w,h) in viewport space:
page.drawRectangle({
  x: vx / scale,
  y: pageHeight - (vy / scale) - (h / scale),  // shift by height: pdf-lib y is the BOTTOM edge
  width:  w / scale,
  height: h / scale,
});
```

Gotchas: (a) pdf-lib's rectangle/image `y` is the **bottom** edge вЂ” subtract element height when converting from a top-left box. (b) If the page has a `/Rotate` value, PDF.js bakes rotation into its viewport but pdf-lib coordinates are in *unrotated* page space вЂ” account for `page.getRotation()` on rotated pages. (c) PDF.js `viewport.convertToPdfPoint(x, y)` gives unscaled, bottom-left-origin PDF points directly, passable straight to pdf-lib.

- pdf-lib вЂ” docs: https://pdf-lib.js.org/
- LoadOptions вЂ” pdf-lib API: https://pdf-lib.js.org/docs/api/interfaces/loadoptions
- PDFForm вЂ” pdf-lib API: https://pdf-lib.js.org/docs/api/classes/pdfform
- Hopding/pdf-lib вЂ” GitHub: https://github.com/Hopding/pdf-lib
- Issue #1326 (encrypted docs): https://github.com/Hopding/pdf-lib/issues/1326
- Issue #1390 (copying encrypted в†’ blank pages): https://github.com/Hopding/pdf-lib/issues/1390
- Issue #814 (flatten PDFDict/PDFStream error): https://github.com/Hopding/pdf-lib/issues/814
- Issue #296 (decoding content streams unsupported): https://github.com/Hopding/pdf-lib/issues/296
- @cantoo/pdf-lib вЂ” npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@cantoo/pdf-lib
- qpdf вЂ” Encryption docs: https://qpdf.readthedocs.io/en/stable/encryption.html
- Build a Node.js PDF editor with pdf-lib (Nutrient): https://www.nutrient.io/blog/how-to-build-a-nodejs-pdf-editor-with-pdflib/

## 5. OCR вЂ” tesseract.js (offline)

### 5.1 Key facts

- `tesseract.js@7.0.0` (released Dec 2025) pairs with `tesseract.js-core@7.x`. v7 is a near-identical superset of v6 (v6's substantive changes were rolled back for Node 14 incompatibility and re-shipped as v7). Requires **Node в‰Ґ16** (в‰Ґ18 for built-in fetch). ~15вЂ“35% faster than v6 via a new `relaxedsimd` WASM build.
- **`createWorker(langs, oem, options, config)`** is the single entry point вЂ” loads language data and initializes in one step. The old `worker.load()`, `worker.loadLanguage()`, `worker.initialize()` calls are **removed** in v6/v7 (delete them). Re-init with different langs/oem via `worker.reinitialize()`.
- **It already runs in a Web Worker.** In a browser/Electron-renderer environment, `createWorker` spins up a real `Worker` internally that loads the WASM core and runs OCR off the main thread. No need to wrap it in your own worker. (`errorHandler`/`logger` callbacks are marshalled back to the main thread.)
- **Output formats changed in v6/v7:** by default `recognize()` returns **only `text`**. Request `blocks` (4th arg) for bounding boxes; `hocr`, `tsv`, `box`, `pdf` are likewise opt-in (the extra formats added 0.25вЂ“0.50s/page).
- For a **searchable text layer over a scanned page**, use the `blocks` JSON tree (`Block в†’ Paragraph в†’ Line в†’ Word в†’ Symbol`), each node carrying `text`, `confidence`, and a pixel `bbox {x0,y0,x1,y1}` in the coordinate space of the image passed to `recognize`.
- **Offline:** point `workerPath`, `corePath`, `langPath` at locally bundled files. `corePath` **must be a directory containing all four core build variants** (not a single `.js` file).

> вљ пёЏ Verify: exact `tesseract.js-core@7` patch version вЂ” core's npm `latest` dist-tag still pointed at **6.1.2** at research time, while `tesseract.js@7.0.0` declares `"tesseract.js-core": "^7.0.0"`. Let `tesseract.js@7` pull in its matching core via the dependency tree; do not pin core to 6.x.

### 5.2 createWorker + progress callback

```ts
import { createWorker, OEM, PSM } from 'tesseract.js';

// createWorker(langs?, oem?, options?, config?)
const worker = await createWorker(
  'eng',          // langs: string | string[] | Lang[]
  OEM.LSTM_ONLY,  // oem = 1 (default/fast; do NOT enable legacy unless you need worker.detect)
  {
    workerPath: '<local>/worker.min.js',
    corePath:   '<local dir with the 4 core files>',
    langPath:   '<local dir of *.traineddata.gz>',
    gzip: true,             // langPath files are <lang>.traineddata.gz (default true)
    cacheMethod: 'none',    // recommended for bundled-offline: skip IndexedDB caching
    logger:       m => console.log(m.status, m.progress),
    errorHandler: e => console.error(e),
  }
);
```

`WorkerOptions` (from `index.d.ts`): `corePath, langPath, cachePath, dataPath, workerPath, cacheMethod ('write'|'readOnly'|'refresh'|'none'), workerBlobURL (bool, default true), gzip (bool, default true), legacyLang (bool), legacyCore (bool), logger, errorHandler`.

`LoggerMessage`:
```ts
type LoggerMessage = {
  jobId: string;
  progress: number;   // 0..1
  status: string;
  userJobId: string;
  workerId: string;
};
```

Observed `status` values (from worker-script source): `'initializing tesseract'`, `'loading language traineddata'`, `'initializing api'`, and during OCR `'recognizing text'` (its `progress` is scaled `(percent-30)/70`, starting mid-way through the engine's internal pass). Gate your progress bar on `status === 'recognizing text'` for the actual OCR phase.

### 5.3 Word/line boxes + text (searchable layer)

Request `blocks` explicitly. Reuse one worker across pages; `terminate()` once at the end.

```ts
const { data } = await worker.recognize(
  imageLike,          // string URL | HTMLImageElement | HTMLCanvasElement | File | Blob | canvas, etc.
  { rotateAuto: true },              // RecognizeOptions (optional)
  { text: true, blocks: true }       // OutputFormats вЂ” MUST opt in to blocks
);

// data.text                  -> full plain text
// data.blocks                -> Block[]  (geometry tree)
for (const block of data.blocks ?? []) {
  for (const para of block.paragraphs) {
    for (const line of para.lines) {
      for (const word of line.words) {
        const { x0, y0, x1, y1 } = word.bbox;   // pixel coords in the recognized image
        const text = word.text;
        const conf = word.confidence;           // 0..100
        // place a positioned <span> at (x0,y0)..(x1,y1) over the page image
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Data structure (verbatim from `index.d.ts`):
- `Bbox { x0; y0; x1; y1 }` вЂ” top-left / bottom-right in **image pixels**.
- `Block { paragraphs: Paragraph[]; text; confidence; bbox; blocktype; page }`
- `Paragraph { lines: Line[]; text; confidence; bbox; is_ltr }`
- `Line { words: Word[]; text; confidence; baseline: {x0,y0,x1,y1}; rowAttributes: {ascenders, descenders, rowHeight}; bbox }`
- `Word { symbols: Symbol[]; choices: Choice[]; text; confidence; bbox; font_name }`
- `Symbol { text; confidence; bbox; is_superscript; is_subscript; is_dropcap }`

For a selectable overlay, position each word/line as an absolutely-positioned, transparent-fill text element. Convert image pixels в†’ CSS by render scale: `cssX = bbox.x0 * (displayedWidth / imageWidth)`. `Line.baseline` and `rowAttributes` help size font/line-height so selection rectangles line up. Alternatively, request `{ pdf: true }` with `RecognizeOptions.pdfTextOnly: true` to have Tesseract emit a ready-made invisible-text PDF layer (`data.pdf` is a `number[]` byte array).

### 5.4 Offline bundling вЂ” exact files

**`corePath` must be a directory containing all four** (from `tesseract.js-core/`):
1. `tesseract-core.wasm.js`
2. `tesseract-core-simd.wasm.js`
3. `tesseract-core-lstm.wasm.js`
4. `tesseract-core-simd-lstm.wasm.js`

Each `*.wasm.js` has a sibling `*.wasm`; copy the whole directory so binaries are co-located. Tesseract.js feature-detects the right variant вЂ” **never** point `corePath` at a single `.js` file. (v7 also adds `relaxedsimd` variants; copying the entire `tesseract.js-core/` dir is the safe move.)

**`workerPath`** в†’ `tesseract.js/dist/worker.min.js`.

**`langPath`** в†’ a directory; Tesseract builds URLs as `langPath + '/' + langCode + '.traineddata.gz'` (do **not** add a trailing `/`). Bundle **`eng.traineddata.gz`** (gzipped) and keep `gzip: true`. If bundling uncompressed `eng.traineddata`, set `gzip: false`. Get language data from `tessdata`/`tessdata_fast` (`https://tessdata.projectnaptha.com/4.0.0/eng.traineddata.gz`) and vendor it locally.

### 5.5 electron-vite / Vite gotchas

1. **Renderer `public/` is the simplest offline host.** electron-vite's renderer root is `src/renderer`, so create `src/renderer/public/`. Contents serve at `/...` in dev and are copied verbatim (no hashing) to renderer output вЂ” ideal for the worker, the 4 core files + `.wasm`, and `eng.traineddata.gz`.
   ```ts
   const base = import.meta.env.DEV ? '/tess' : './tess'; // adjust for packaged file:// base
   const worker = await createWorker('eng', 1, {
     workerPath: `${base}/worker.min.js`,
     corePath:   `${base}/core`,           // directory with the 4 files
     langPath:   `${base}/lang`,           // contains eng.traineddata.gz
     gzip: true, cacheMethod: 'none',
   });
   ```
   Auto-copy from `node_modules` into `public/` with a small prebuild script (survives upgrades).

2. **Packaged `file://` base path is the #1 footgun.** A packaged renderer loads over `file://`, where root-absolute `/tess/...` resolves to the filesystem root, not your app. Use relative paths (`./tess/...`) in production, or resolve via main and hand the renderer an absolute `file://` URL through `contextBridge`/IPC. Confirm `vite build` `base` is `./` for the renderer.

3. **Vite production `browser`-field bug** ([vite#2444](https://github.com/vitejs/vite/issues/2444)): tesseract.js's `package.json` `browser` mapping resolves in dev but can pull Node-only code into a production renderer build, throwing at runtime. Workarounds: `patch-package` the `import.meta`/`require` switch line, add an explicit `resolve.alias`, or mark external and load `worker.min.js` purely as a static asset. **Test the production build, not just dev.**

4. **Do not let Vite transform the worker/wasm.** Treat them as opaque static files (public dir, or `import workerUrl from '.../worker.min.js?url'`). Avoid `new Worker(new URL(...))` rewriting and Vite's `?init`/`?loader` wasm handling for the core вЂ” Tesseract loads its own WASM via `corePath` at runtime; you only need the files on disk.

5. **`assetsInclude` for `.traineddata`** вЂ” if importing traineddata as a module asset instead of `public/`, add `assetsInclude: ['**/*.traineddata', '**/*.traineddata.gz']` to the renderer Vite config and use `?url` imports. The `public/` approach sidesteps this entirely (recommended for offline).

6. **CSP / sandbox**: the worker runs in the renderer's web-worker context; ensure CSP allows `worker-src blob:` since `workerBlobURL` defaults to `true`. If CSP forbids `blob:` workers, set `workerBlobURL: false` so it loads `worker.min.js` directly from `workerPath`. (Coordinate with the CSP in В§2.6.)

7. Official Electron reference app: `github.com/Balearica/tesseract.js-electron` (webpack-based, but shows asset-copy + path wiring).

### 5.6 Recommended approach: PDF.js в†’ image в†’ OCR

```ts
import * as pdfjsLib from 'pdfjs-dist';
pdfjsLib.GlobalWorkerOptions.workerSrc = './pdfjs/pdf.worker.min.mjs'; // bundled offline

const pdf = await pdfjsLib.getDocument({ data: bytes }).promise;
const worker = await createWorker('eng', 1, { /* offline paths as above */ });

for (let n = 1; n <= pdf.numPages; n++) {
  const page = await pdf.getPage(n);
  const scale = 300 / 72;                       // ~4.17x вЂ” render at >= 300 DPI for OCR quality
  const viewport = page.getViewport({ scale });
  const canvas = document.createElement('canvas');
  canvas.width = viewport.width;
  canvas.height = viewport.height;
  const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d')!;
  await page.render({ canvasContext: ctx, viewport }).promise;

  const { data } = await worker.recognize(canvas, {}, { text: true, blocks: true });
  // data.blocks bboxes are in CANVAS pixels (= rendered viewport space).
  // To overlay on a differently-sized display image, scale by displayWidth / canvas.width.
}
await worker.terminate();
```

Notes:
- **Render high-res** (~300 DPI, not the 72 DPI PDF default). Set `user_defined_dpi: '300'` via `setParameters`/config to silence "Invalid resolution 0 dpi" and aid layout analysis.
- **Keep the same canvas/viewport** you OCR'd as the display image (or store its width/height) for exact bboxв†’screen scaling.
- **PDF.js also offline-bundled**: vendor `pdf.worker.min.mjs` into `public/`, set `GlobalWorkerOptions.workerSrc` locally; pass the PDF as bytes (`{ data: Uint8Array }`) so nothing hits the network.
- Reuse one Tesseract worker across pages; `terminate()` once. Periodically recreate the worker on very large batches (the v6/v7 memory-growth fix landed, but maintainers still advise periodic refresh).

### Sources

- tesseract.js API docs: https://github.com/naptha/tesseract.js/blob/master/docs/api.md
- tesseract.js local-installation docs: https://github.com/naptha/tesseract.js/blob/master/docs/local-installation.md
- tesseract.js TS definitions (`src/index.d.ts`): https://github.com/naptha/tesseract.js/blob/master/src/index.d.ts
- tesseract.js worker-script (status strings): https://github.com/naptha/tesseract.js/blob/master/src/worker-script/index.js
- npm registry: `registry.npmjs.org/tesseract.js/latest` (7.0.0); `registry.npmjs.org/tesseract.js-core/latest` (6.1.2 dist-tag)
- Releases / v6 changes: https://github.com/naptha/tesseract.js/releases В· https://github.com/naptha/tesseract.js/issues/993
- electron-vite Asset Handling: https://electron-vite.org/guide/assets.html
- Vite production `browser` field bug: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/issues/2444
- Official Electron example: https://github.com/Balearica/tesseract.js-electron
- PDF.js (`pdfjs-dist`): https://github.com/mozilla/pdf.js

## 6. qpdf Sidecar + Secure Redaction

### 6.1 Obtaining the binary & license

- Latest stable qpdf **12.3.2** (12.3.x). Releases at `github.com/qpdf/qpdf/releases`, mirrored on SourceForge.
- License **Apache-2.0** (confirmed via Homebrew, MSYS2, project `LICENSE.txt`/`NOTICE.md`). Copyright (c) 2005-2021 Jay Berkenbilt; 2022-2026 Jay Berkenbilt and Manfred Holger. (Versions before 7 were Artistic License 2.0; does not affect current distribution.) Permissive and bundling-safe вЂ” ship the `LICENSE.txt`/`NOTICE` file with the app.
- **Windows asset:** `qpdf-12.3.2-bin-msvc64.zip` (highest-performance). **NOT a single self-contained static exe** вЂ” the zip's `bin/` has `qpdf.exe` **plus several DLLs** (`qpdf<N>.dll` + zlib/jpeg etc.) and **depends on the MSVC Visual C++ runtime**. Bundle the entire `bin/` directory and either rely on the system VC++ runtime or ship the VC++ redistributable DLLs alongside.
- **Alternative:** `qpdf-12.3.2-bin-mingw64.zip` ships **fewer DLLs and does NOT require a VC++ runtime DLL** вЂ” often easier to bundle cleanly, at a slight performance cost. Either way, ship the whole `bin/` folder, not just `.exe`.

> вљ пёЏ Verify (trade-off): For a consumer Electron app wanting zero external runtime assumptions, the **mingw64 build is the more robust bundling choice**; msvc64 is faster. Pick per your priorities.

### 6.2 Bundling with electron-builder

- Use `extraResources` вЂ” files land **outside** the ASAR archive as real files on disk (required; you cannot spawn an exe from inside ASAR).
- `from` in `extraResources` is relative to the **project root** (differs from `files`, where `from` is relative to the app dir).
- Resolve at runtime by branching on `app.isPackaged`: packaged в†’ `process.resourcesPath`; dev в†’ a project-root path.

### 6.3 child_process safety

- Use `spawn` with an **args array** (never a single command string, never `shell: true`) вЂ” eliminates shell-injection because args go directly to OS exec, not a shell. Malicious filenames/passwords are inert as argv entries.
- qpdf's `--` separator ends option parsing so a filename can't be misread as a flag.

### 6.4 Secure redaction (the important part)

- **qpdf does NOT redact.** It is a structural transformer (encrypt/decrypt/linearize/repair/check). No concept of "remove the text under this rectangle." A black box (annotation or vector rect) leaves the original text/image objects intact вЂ” searchable, copyable, extractable in any other viewer. The #1 redaction failure.
- **Whiteout / black box = cover only; data remains.** Never present this as redaction.
- **Honest, safe approach for a consumer editor: rasterize.** Render the affected page (or at minimum the affected region, then re-composite) to a raster image at sufficient DPI, paint the redaction fill on the raster, rebuild the PDF page from that image. This **destroys the underlying text/vector objects** (only pixels remain) вЂ” strongest assurance, and the only method safe to ship to non-expert users.
- Object-level removal (surgically deleting content-stream operators) is more precise and keeps text searchable elsewhere on the page, but is **error-prone** (glyph-position leaks, partial removal). For a consumer product, rasterizing the page is the defensible default.
- Even "correct" object removal can leak: the *Glyph Positions Break PDF Text Redaction* research (Univ. of Wisconsin) showed layout metadata can reconstruct hidden text. Also beware **length leaks** (box width reveals character count) and **metadata/hidden layers** вЂ” strip XMP/metadata, attachments, and OCGs after redaction.
- **License-clean rendering for rasterization:** use **PDFium** (BSD-3-Clause / Apache-2.0). Avoid AGPL/GPL options вЂ” **MuPDF (AGPL)** and **Ghostscript (AGPL)** are not safe for a closed-source consumer app. PDFium binaries: `bblanchon/pdfium-binaries`. For Node/Electron, `@hyzyla/pdfium` (WASM wrapper, zero deps) or `@embedpdf/pdfium` (MIT wrapper).

> вљ пёЏ Verify: `@hyzyla/pdfium` wrapper license вЂ” confirm on its repo. `@embedpdf/pdfium` is MIT.

**Recommended redaction pipeline:** PDFium renders page в†’ app paints redaction fills on the raster в†’ rebuild page as image-only PDF в†’ qpdf linearizes/sanitizes/encrypts the result.

### 6.5 electron-builder config (bundle whole qpdf bin folder)

```yaml
extraResources:
  - from: "resources/qpdf/win"   # contains qpdf.exe + ALL its DLLs
    to: "qpdf"
    filter: ["**/*"]
```

Runtime path resolution (Electron main):

```js
const path = require('path');
const { app } = require('electron');

function qpdfPath() {
  const dir = app.isPackaged
    ? path.join(process.resourcesPath, 'qpdf')              // packaged
    : path.join(__dirname, '..', 'resources', 'qpdf', 'win'); // dev (project root)
  return path.join(dir, 'qpdf.exe');
}
```

Safe invocation with `spawn` (args array, no shell):

```js
const { spawn } = require('child_process');

function runQpdf(args) {
  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    const child = spawn(qpdfPath(), args, { shell: false, windowsHide: true });
    let stderr = '';
    child.stderr.on('data', d => (stderr += d));
    child.on('error', reject);
    child.on('close', code =>
      code === 0 || code === 3   // 3 = warnings only, output still produced
        ? resolve()
        : reject(new Error(`qpdf exited ${code}: ${stderr}`))
    );
  });
}
```

### 6.6 Exact qpdf CLI (each as an args array)

```js
// Encrypt: AES-256, set user + owner password + permissions
// CLI: qpdf --encrypt USER OWNER 256 --print=none --modify=none --extract=n -- in.pdf out.pdf
['--encrypt', userPw, ownerPw, '256',
 '--print=none', '--modify=none', '--extract=n',
 '--', inPath, outPath]

// Open-password-only (INSECURE; empty owner requires --allow-insecure for 256-bit)
['--encrypt', userPw, '', '256', '--allow-insecure', '--', inPath, outPath]

// Decrypt with known password
['--decrypt', `--password=${pw}`, inPath, outPath]

// Repair (rewrite reconstructs xref/object structure). Separate output:
[inPath, outPath]
// Or in place (OVERWRITES input вЂ” back up first):
['--replace-input', inPath]

// Linearize (web-optimized)
['--linearize', inPath, outPath]

// Check structure / report encryption + issues
['--check', inPath]
```

Flag notes:
- Key length **256** always uses AES (the only secure choice; avoid 40-bit and RC4-128).
- `--print=full|low|none`, `--modify=all|annotate|form|assembly|none`, `--extract=y|n` set the permission bits (stored in `P`).
- For 256-bit, an empty owner password is insecure and **requires `--allow-insecure`** вЂ” restrictions won't be enforced and the file opens without a password.
- `--decrypt` with no `--password` often works on files encrypted with an empty owner password.
- Combine ops in one pass (e.g. add `--linearize` to an `--encrypt` command).

### 6.7 Gotchas

- **Bundling just `qpdf.exe` will fail at runtime** on the MSVC build вЂ” it needs sibling DLLs + VC++ runtime. Ship the entire `bin/` folder; prefer mingw64 to avoid the VC++ runtime dependency.
- `extraResources.from` is relative to **project root**, not the app dir вЂ” a frequent "FileSet from path not found" mistake.
- `app.isPackaged` can return `false` under some Vite dev setups; have a fallback (`NODE_ENV`, dev-server env var).
- qpdf exit code **3 means warnings only** вЂ” output is still written; don't treat as failure.
- `--replace-input` overwrites the original вЂ” always copy first.
- **Redaction:** a black rectangle is not redaction; data remains. Rasterize is the safe default. After redaction, strip metadata/attachments/hidden layers and validate on a clean viewer (attempt copy/select-all/search/OCR).
- Owner password is trivially bypassable by design вЂ” treat permission restrictions as deterrence, not security. Only the **user (open) password** with AES-256 provides real confidentiality.
- **License hygiene:** keep MuPDF and Ghostscript (both AGPL) out of the build; use PDFium (BSD/Apache-2.0). Ship qpdf's and PDFium's license/NOTICE files.

### Sources

- Releases В· qpdf/qpdf: https://github.com/qpdf/qpdf/releases
- Release Notes вЂ” qpdf 12.3.2: https://qpdf.readthedocs.io/en/stable/release-notes.html
- README-what-to-download.md: https://github.com/qpdf/qpdf/blob/main/README-what-to-download.md
- License вЂ” qpdf: https://qpdf.readthedocs.io/en/stable/license.html В· LICENSE.txt: https://github.com/qpdf/qpdf/blob/main/LICENSE.txt В· NOTICE.md: https://github.com/qpdf/qpdf/blob/main/NOTICE.md
- PDF Encryption вЂ” qpdf: https://qpdf.readthedocs.io/en/stable/encryption.html
- Running qpdf (CLI): https://qpdf.readthedocs.io/en/stable/cli.html В· Options: https://qpdf.readthedocs.io/en/stable/qpdf-options.html
- Application Contents | electron-builder: https://www.electron.build/docs/contents/ В· Common Config: https://www.electron.build/configuration.html
- Why Drawing Black Boxes Is Not Real Redaction вЂ” RedactVault: https://www.redactvault.com/blog/why-drawing-black-boxes-over-a-pdf-is-not-real-redaction
- Deep Research on PDF Redaction Failures вЂ” Argelius Labs: https://www.argeliuslabs.com/deep-research-on-pdf-redaction-failures-and-security-risks-exploits-and-best-practices/
- LICENSE вЂ” pdfium (Google): https://pdfium.googlesource.com/pdfium/+/main/LICENSE В· bblanchon/pdfium-binaries: https://github.com/bblanchon/pdfium-binaries
- @hyzyla/pdfium вЂ” npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@hyzyla/pdfium В· @embedpdf/pdfium вЂ” npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@embedpdf/pdfium

## 7. Packaging, Auto-Update, E2E & CI

### 7.1 Key facts

- **electron-builder** packages; **electron-updater** is its companion runtime auto-update library. They version independently (builder `26.x`, updater `6.x`). electron-builder auto-generates `latest.yml` (Windows) and, with `--publish`, uploads it + the installer + `.blockmap` to a GitHub Release.
- **Windows file associations require `nsis.perMachine: true`.** electron-builder throws a build error if `fileAssociations` is set without it. True per-extension *opt-in* is NOT a first-class flag вЂ” done via a custom NSIS `include` script or runtime registration (see Gotchas).
- **electron-updater** does not need `setFeedURL` вЂ” electron-builder bakes `app-update.yml` into resources at build time from `publish`. Make checks opt-in: do NOT call `checkForUpdates()` automatically; gate behind a menu item / IPC and set `autoDownload = false`.
- **Playwright** tests Electron via `_electron.launch({ args: [mainEntry] })`. Point `args[0]` at the **built** main entry (`out/main/index.js`), not source. On `windows-latest` no xvfb needed (desktop session present; xvfb is Linux-only).
- **Vitest** runs the renderer (jsdom) cleanly. Use a dedicated `vitest.config.ts` (different schema from `electron.vite.config.ts`).

Node engines: Playwright 1.61 supports Node 22.x/24.x/26.x; electron-builder needs Node в‰Ґ14 but use Node 20/22 LTS for CI.

### 7.2 `electron-builder.yml`

```yaml
# electron-builder.yml
appId: com.commonhype.myapp
productName: MyApp
copyright: Copyright В© 2026 CommonHype

directories:
  output: release/${version}
  buildResources: build        # icons, custom NSIS scripts

files:
  - out/**/*
  - package.json

extraResources:
  - from: vendor/qpdf/win/         # bundled qpdf binaries + DLLs
    to: qpdf                       # -> resources/qpdf/qpdf.exe at runtime
    filter:
      - "**/*"

win:
  icon: build/icon.ico
  target:
    - target: nsis
      arch: [x64]
    - target: portable
      arch: [x64]

portable:
  artifactName: ${productName}-${version}-portable.exe
  unicode: true

nsis:
  oneClick: false                          # assisted installer (Next/Next/Finish)
  perMachine: true                         # REQUIRED for file associations
  allowElevation: true
  allowToChangeInstallationDirectory: true
  createDesktopShortcut: true
  createStartMenuShortcut: true
  shortcutName: MyApp
  deleteAppDataOnUninstall: false
  runAfterFinish: true
  installerIcon: build/icon.ico
  uninstallerIcon: build/icon.ico
  # include: build/installer.nsh           # for opt-in associations (see Gotchas)

fileAssociations:
  - ext: pdf
    name: PDF Document
    description: PDF opened with MyApp
    icon: build/pdf.ico          # build-resources-relative; falls back to app icon
    role: Viewer                 # macOS only; harmless on Win

publish:
  - provider: github
    owner: commonhypeaz
    repo: myapp
    # releaseType: release       # or 'draft' / 'prerelease'
```

NSIS option meanings: `oneClick:false` в†’ assisted (multi-page) installer; `perMachine:true` в†’ installs for all users (mandatory for associations and to register the `.pdf` handler globally); `allowToChangeInstallationDirectory` only applies with `oneClick:false`; `deleteAppDataOnUninstall` wipes userData on uninstall.

Runtime path to bundled qpdf (matches В§6.5; note this variant resolves dev under `app.getAppPath()/vendor/...`):
```ts
import { app } from 'electron'
import path from 'node:path'

const qpdfPath = app.isPackaged
  ? path.join(process.resourcesPath, 'qpdf', 'qpdf.exe')
  : path.join(app.getAppPath(), 'vendor', 'qpdf', 'win', 'qpdf.exe')
```

### 7.3 electron-updater вЂ” main-process flow (opt-in, GitHub provider)

```ts
// src/main/updater.ts  (electron-vite main process, ESM)
import { app, ipcMain, dialog, BrowserWindow } from 'electron'
import electronUpdater from 'electron-updater'      // CJS interop workaround
import log from 'electron-log'

const { autoUpdater } = electronUpdater             // destructure for ESM

export function initUpdater(win: BrowserWindow) {
  autoUpdater.logger = log
  log.transports.file.level = 'info'

  // Opt-in: do NOT auto-download or auto-check on launch.
  autoUpdater.autoDownload = false
  autoUpdater.autoInstallOnAppQuit = true

  autoUpdater.on('checking-for-update', () => win.webContents.send('update:checking'))
  autoUpdater.on('update-available', (info) => win.webContents.send('update:available', info))
  autoUpdater.on('update-not-available', (info) => win.webContents.send('update:none', info))
  autoUpdater.on('download-progress', (p) => win.webContents.send('update:progress', p))
  autoUpdater.on('update-downloaded', (info) => win.webContents.send('update:downloaded', info))
  autoUpdater.on('error', (err) => win.webContents.send('update:error', String(err)))

  // Opt-in IPC: renderer (e.g. a "Check for updates" menu item) triggers these.
  ipcMain.handle('update:check', () => autoUpdater.checkForUpdates())
  ipcMain.handle('update:download', () => autoUpdater.downloadUpdate())
  ipcMain.handle('update:install', () => autoUpdater.quitAndInstall())
}
```

- `app-update.yml` is generated by electron-builder from `publish:` вЂ” **do not** call `setFeedURL`.
- `latest.yml` is generated at build time (version, sha512, file size, blockmap ref) and uploaded by `--publish`. The updater fetches `latest.yml` from the GitHub Release to decide if an update exists.
- For dev testing without packaging, add `dev-app-update.yml` at project root mirroring the publish config:
  ```yaml
  provider: github
  owner: commonhypeaz
  repo: myapp
  ```
- Type-safe alternative to `.on(...)`: `autoUpdater.signals.updateDownloaded(() => { ... })`.

### 7.4 Playwright E2E for Electron

```ts
// playwright.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from '@playwright/test'

export default defineConfig({
  testDir: './e2e',
  fullyParallel: false,        // Electron launches are heavy; keep serial
  timeout: 60_000,
  reporter: 'list',
})
```

```ts
// e2e/app.spec.ts
import { test, expect, _electron as electron } from '@playwright/test'
import path from 'node:path'
import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url'

const __dirname = path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))
const MAIN_ENTRY = path.join(__dirname, '..', 'out', 'main', 'index.js') // electron-vite output

test('app opens a window with the expected title', async () => {
  const app = await electron.launch({
    args: [MAIN_ENTRY],
    env: { ...process.env, NODE_ENV: 'production' },
  })

  const window = await app.firstWindow()
  await window.waitForLoadState('domcontentloaded')

  expect(await window.title()).toContain('MyApp')
  await expect(window.locator('#root')).toBeVisible()

  // Run code in the main process
  const isPackaged = await app.evaluate(({ app }) => app.isPackaged)
  expect(typeof isPackaged).toBe('boolean')

  // Stub native dialogs deterministically (Playwright cannot click OS dialogs)
  await app.evaluate(({ dialog }) => {
    dialog.showOpenDialog = async () => ({ canceled: false, filePaths: ['C:\\tmp\\x.pdf'] })
  })

  await app.close()
})
```

Notes:
- Build before testing: `electron-vite build` must run first so `out/main/index.js` exists.
- `executablePath` is optional вЂ” omitting it uses the Electron binary in `node_modules/.bin/electron` to run your built JS. To test the *packaged* `.exe`, set `executablePath` to the installed binary.
- **CI headless:** on `windows-latest` no xvfb needed. Playwright drives Electron via CDP regardless of headed/headless. Ensure the `EnableNodeCliInspectArguments` fuse is not disabled, or launch will time out.

### 7.5 Vitest + React Testing Library (renderer)

```ts
// vitest.config.ts  (separate from electron.vite.config.ts)
/// <reference types="vitest/config" />
import { defineConfig } from 'vitest/config'
import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'
import path from 'node:path'

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [react()],
  resolve: {
    alias: { '@renderer': path.resolve(__dirname, 'src/renderer/src') },
  },
  test: {
    globals: true,
    environment: 'jsdom',
    setupFiles: './vitest.setup.ts',
    include: ['src/renderer/**/*.{test,spec}.{ts,tsx}'],
  },
})
```

```ts
// vitest.setup.ts
import { afterEach } from 'vitest'
import { cleanup } from '@testing-library/react'
import '@testing-library/jest-dom/vitest'   // registers matchers + TS types

afterEach(() => cleanup())
```

```jsonc
// tsconfig (renderer) compilerOptions
"types": ["vitest/globals", "@testing-library/jest-dom", "vite/client"]
```

```tsx
// src/renderer/src/App.test.tsx
import { render, screen } from '@testing-library/react'
import App from './App'

it('renders heading', () => {
  render(<App />)
  expect(screen.getByRole('heading', { name: /myapp/i })).toBeInTheDocument()
})
```

### 7.6 GitHub Actions вЂ” CI (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`)

```yaml
name: CI
on:
  push: { branches: [main] }
  pull_request:

jobs:
  build-test:
    runs-on: windows-latest
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        node-version: [20, 22]
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}
          cache: npm
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run lint
      - run: npm run typecheck
      - run: npm test          # vitest run
      - run: npm run build     # electron-vite build (+ optional electron-builder --dir)
```

### 7.7 GitHub Actions вЂ” Release (`.github/workflows/release.yml`)

```yaml
name: Release
on:
  push:
    tags: ['v*']

permissions:
  contents: write           # needed to create the GitHub Release

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: windows-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: npm
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run build                       # electron-vite build
      - run: npx --no-install electron-builder --win --publish always
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}  # electron-updater expects GH_TOKEN
```

`--publish always` uploads the NSIS installer, portable exe, `latest.yml`, and `.blockmap` to the tagged GitHub Release. The built-in `GITHUB_TOKEN` is sufficient for same-repo releases (no PAT needed).

### 7.8 Gotchas

- **Opt-in `.pdf` association is not built-in.** `fileAssociations` + `perMachine:true` registers the handler for all users unconditionally at install. For genuine opt-in: (a) omit `fileAssociations` and present a checkbox via a custom NSIS macro (`nsis.include: build/installer.nsh` with `customInstall`/`customUnInstall` writing `HKLM\Software\Classes` keys conditionally), or (b) register at runtime via `app.setAsDefaultProtocolClient` / Windows `assoc` APIs only when the user opts in. Even a correct config often only adds MyApp to the "Open with" list вЂ” Windows 10/11 deliberately blocks installers from silently seizing the default `.pdf` handler; the user must confirm in Settings.
- **`perMachine:true` forces UAC elevation** at install; with `oneClick:false` you get the assisted installer + directory chooser.
- **ESM import of electron-updater**: `import { autoUpdater } from 'electron-updater'` can break under ESM (package is CJS). Use `import electronUpdater from 'electron-updater'; const { autoUpdater } = electronUpdater`.
- **Playwright main entry**: pointing `args` at `src/main/index.ts` (source TS) fails вЂ” Electron runs JS. Always target `out/main/index.js` and build in CI before the Playwright step.
- **electron-builder version drift**: builder `26.x` and updater `6.x` are independent; keep `electron-updater` as a runtime `dependency` (so it's bundled), `electron-builder` as a devDependency.
- **Code signing (OSS)**: Windows builds are unsigned by default; users get SmartScreen "Unknown publisher" warnings, and `electron-updater` skips signature validation when no signature is present (updates still work). For OSS this is acceptable вЂ” document it. To sign, set `win.certificateFile`/`certificatePassword` (or Azure Trusted Signing / hardware token via `win.signtoolOptions`); never commit certs вЂ” inject via CI secrets. Self-signed certs do not remove SmartScreen warnings.
- **`@playwright/test@1.61` uses Chrome for Testing builds** (switched from Chromium at 1.57); functionally identical for Electron CDP testing.

> вљ пёЏ Verify: **GitHub blockmap differential-download quirk** вЂ” differential downloads sometimes fail because GitHub can't serve the *old* release's blockmap from the latest URL. If hit, set the blockmap base URL manually or disable differential downloads. Unconfirmed whether still required in updater 6.8.9 вЂ” verify against your release flow.

> вљ пёЏ Verify: The exact opt-in association NSIS macro is environment-specific and not a documented first-class flag.

## 8. Cross-Cutting: Recommended PDF Pipeline

A full editor combines the libraries by strength, never expecting any one to do everything:

```
PDF.js (pdfjs-dist)   в†’  render pages, text layer, search, viewport coords (renderer)
        в†“
pdf-lib               в†’  page ops, drawing, AcroForms, metadata (add/structure only)
        в†“
tesseract.js          в†’  OCR scanned pages (PDF.js canvas в†’ blocks в†’ searchable text layer)
        в†“
PDFium / mutool       в†’  TRUE redaction & content removal (rasterize affected pages)
        в†“
qpdf (sidecar)        в†’  encryption (AES-256), linearization, sanitization, repair, check
```

Hard division of labor (do not violate):
- **Encryption / password protection** в†’ qpdf (pdf-lib cannot produce encrypted output).
- **Content-stream / glyph editing & text extraction** в†’ PDF.js / PDFium / mutool (pdf-lib can only *add* content).
- **True redaction** в†’ rasterize via PDFium, then qpdf sanitize/linearize. Drawing a black box is NOT redaction.
- **Coordinate bridging** between PDF.js (top-left) and pdf-lib (bottom-left) в†’ use `viewport.convertToPdfPoint(x, y)` or the conversion in В§4.9.
- **License hygiene** в†’ PDFium (BSD/Apache-2.0) only; keep MuPDF and Ghostscript (AGPL) out of a closed-source build; ship qpdf and PDFium LICENSE/NOTICE files.
